methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Copenhagen Atomics is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised $25M+ to date. The valuation benchmark universe comprises 11 peers across nuclear energy—3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, and General Matter. This fragmented comp set reflects the nascent state of the frontier nuclear sector.

A peer-median approach is not applicable due to insufficient narrow comparables within Copenhagen Atomics' specific stage and valuation tier. The nuclear energy peer universe, while populated, lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage companies with reliable data to support a robust median benchmark. Consequently, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a standard 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier to the $25M total raised proxy, implying a post-Series A valuation of $50M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, with a median of $50M. This range reflects the inherent widening around a single-method estimate, as typical in data-constrained early-stage sectors. Key uncertainties include the absence of meaningful peer comparables at Copenhagen Atomics' stage, limiting calibration of the applied multiplier; the company's specific technology maturity and capital intensity relative to the broader nuclear cohort; and the evolving regulatory and financing landscape for advanced nuclear. A higher-confidence range would require either materialization of narrow peer exits or accumulation of additional Series A+ nuclear energy datapoints.
